tool.name: diffseq
tool.summary: compare two similar sequences and report contiguous difference blocks from a deterministic global alignment

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `diffseq` compares exactly one sequence from each input, computes a deterministic global alignment, and reports contiguous mismatch or indel blocks in a governed table. EMBOSS-RS v1 treats the alignment as the comparison substrate and emits typed block coordinates instead of a prose-only narrative.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts exactly one local sequence record from each of two inputs. Optional `--gap-open` and `--gap-extend` flags tune the deterministic global-alignment penalties. Both inputs must resolve to a compatible nucleotide or protein comparison mode.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The output is a stable table with one row per contiguous difference block. Each row reports a classification, optional 1-based inclusive coordinates in both sequences, and the aligned block segments. When the sequences are identical, the table is empty and the summary states that zero difference blocks were found.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs diffseq`. Validation currently covers one committed substitution case through the Rust tool and service layers.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release is a deterministic block report, not a full recreation of historical EMBOSS difference narration. It does not attempt feature-aware commentary, biological effect interpretation, or alternative comparison heuristics beyond the governed global-alignment path.

artifacts.label: Diffseq left FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed one-record FASTA fixture used as the left-hand diffseq input.

artifacts.label: Diffseq right FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed one-record FASTA fixture used as the right-hand diffseq input.

examples.title: Report one substitution block between two similar sequences
examples.description: Runs `diffseq` against two committed one-record FASTA fixtures that differ at exactly one position.

expected_outputs.label: Difference block table
expected_outputs.description: One row is reported with a `substitution` classification and matching 1-based block coordinates at position `5`.
